items.title: A guide to targeting with Sponsored Products
items.excerpt: Discover tips to help you drive sales through targeting with Sponsored Products.
items.body: Guide A simple guide to effective targeting with Sponsored Products Tips to help you drive sales through targeting with Sponsored Products. Table of contents 1 . An introduction to Sponsored Products targeting What is targeting? 2 . Targeting options with Sponsored Products Automatic targeting Manual targeting Negative targeting Deciding which targeting strategy 3 . Automatic targeting with Sponsored Products Remember this When to choose automatic targeting Just getting started with Sponsored Products? Already have active manual campaigns? 4 . Manual targeting (keywords) with Sponsored Products More about match types Remember this Top tips Keyword types Do your research To note, for Kindle Direct Publishing (KDP) authors and book vendors Choose keyword targeting when: 5 . Manual targeting (product) with Sponsored Products Remember this Choose product targeting when: 6 . Negative targeting with Sponsored Products Consider adding the following as negative keywords Remember this 7 . Setting up strategic targeting for Sponsored Products campaigns How to be strategic Start by defining your business goals Next, choose your campaign strategy 6 tips to remember How campaign structure can impact advertising performance 8 . Sponsored Products targeting checklist An introduction to Sponsored Products targeting Strong targeting is crucial to build a solid foundation for your Sponsored Products campaigns, and can help to match shoppers intents to your products. What is targeting? Targeting is the way you define the context in which you want your ads to appear. That can be done in multiple ways. The most common is the use of keywords that match your ad with the shopping queries customers use when looking for something online. This guide will explore everything you need to know about...

items.title: SP-API Release Notes
items.excerpt: Selling Partner API new features, software updates, and changelog.
items.body: For releases prior to January 19, 2022, refer to Announcements in the Selling Partner API GitHub repo . May 27, 2026 API changes Listings Restrictions API v2021-08-01 Added the optional productType query parameter to the getListingsRestrictions operation. You can use this parameter along with a brand name to evaluate GTIN exemption restrictions for a specific product type. Notifications API v1 Added the filterExpression field to the createSubscription operation. You can use this field to filter events before notification delivery. Filters are applied to the notification payload using Common Expression Language (CEL) syntax. Added DeliveryTipChange as a supported order change type to the OrderChangeType notification schema. You can use this to track the status of a delivery tip for an order. Orders API v0 The following updates apply to veterinary products: Added the approvedAlternativeDetails field to the VerificationDetails object in the updateVerificationStatus operation. You can use this field to submit pre-approved alternative product attributes. Added the interimStatusDetail field to the VerificationDetails object in the updateVerificationStatus operation. You can use this field to provide granular progress updates while an order is pending verification to indicate that a veterinarian has been contacted. Added the ValidInterimStatusCodes field to the RegulatedOrderVerificationStatus object in the getOrderRegulatedInfo response. This field returns the list of supported interim status codes and customer-facing descriptions that you can use when you provide an interimStatusDetail . Orders API v2026-01-01 Added the breakdowns array to the getOrder and searchOrders operations. You can use this attribute to show an order's proceeds breakdown, including delivery tip...

items.title: 利用一个指令快速分析非标品类目的不同方向
items.excerpt: 很多亚马逊卖家做选品时习惯思考或者问这样一个问题：这个类目能不能做？这个问题其实很容易把人带偏，尤其是面对非标品类目，因为非标品类目很多时候混杂着非常多不同的销售方向。 这次以美国站 Amazon 的 Cupcake Stands 类目为例，用 Top100 榜单中的标题、五点描述、价格、销量、Review、上架时间等数据，简单快速拆解下非标品类目的分析过程。 第一，Cupcake Stands 不是一个单一市场 从类目名看，Cupc...

items.title: 描述一下我的亚马逊履历（无美化），希望前辈们帮我判断一下我的工作经历，是否够资格在南京/杭州/苏州拿到年包35w的offer？
items.excerpt: 至今入行亚马逊 3 年，之前有 5 年其他行业经验（创业 + 主管）。主要描述一下我的亚马逊履历，履历中的数据无美化，写正式简历的时候我会再美化增加一点点。 23 年 6 月入行助理，美国 FBA，精细化精铺运营，低至中客单价（$9.9-$80），标品，负责约 40 个 SKU 左右，链接数量 10 条左右。3 个月转正，年底内部评级中级运营，24 年初取得内部涨薪 10% 名额，全部门一年内涨薪人数 5-8 人（小道消息，信息拼凑出...

items.title: 亚马逊怎么抄作业 之 亚马逊竞品调研
items.excerpt: 大家学生时代都抄过作业吧，到了工作上，做啥事，第一时间也会想到抄作业，很多新人朋友刚做亚马逊的时候也经常问我：你做的是什么产品，链接和店铺可以发给我看看吗？我一般会问他你做什么产品，我看看我有没有做过相似的，我链接发给你。当然，我是根据他做的产品，我去找他类目里的头部卖家，或者跟他产品一模一样的链接发给他，跟他说你去抄他的就行。 友情提示：这里说的"抄"产品，是教你"学"对手的思路。毕竟，站在巨人的肩膀上，你才能看得更远，也可能摔得更...

items.title: # 运营案例分析 # 月销400+的链接广告应该如何调整？最近1个月广告突然变差，点击远多于转化ACOS飙高，大词组还比长尾组更差，怎么救？
items.excerpt: 这个链接大概月销订单量在400+，在小类排名120左右。最近一个月的时间的广告突然变差。 词基本上都有转化，但是点击远远多于转化，导致ACOS很高。尝试调竞价和广告位置竞价百分比，都没有得到很大的效果。 链接整体出单不稳定，时好时坏。目前这个链接打了两个SP广告组，一个自动广告组。一组是大词组，一组是长尾组，大词组转化对比长尾组要更差。希望有大佬能够指导一下。

items.title: #753 - The 6-Figure Amazon Launch Sprint
items.excerpt: A legacy brand had never sold on Amazon. One launch strategy later, it hit six figures in year one. What made the difference? Here’s how… ► Watch The Podcasts On Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/@Helium10SeriousSellersPodcast?sub_confirmation=1► Instagram: instagram.com/serioussellerspodcast ► Free Amazon Seller Chrome Extension: https://h10.me/extension ► Sign Up For Helium 10: https://h10.me/signup (Use SSP10 To S...
items.body: A legacy brand had never sold on Amazon. One launch strategy later, it hit six figures in year one. What made the difference? Here’s how… ► Watch The Podcasts On Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/@Helium10SeriousSellersPodcast?sub_confirmation=1 ► Instagram: instagram.com/serioussellerspodcast ► Free Amazon Seller Chrome Extension: https://h10.me/extension ► Sign Up For Helium 10: https://h10.me/signup (Use SSP10 To Save 10% For Life) ► Learn How To Sell on Amazon: https://h10.me/ft What does it take to bring a decades-old retail brand onto Amazon and turn it into a six-figure channel in its first year? In this episode, Bradley Sutton welcomes Spencer Gordon, an e-commerce operator who has built Amazon businesses from the ground up, including a premium beverage storefront and, most recently, the Amazon channel for Leanin Tree Greeting Cards. Spencer shares how he went from working in his family’s logistics and beverage business to launching Amazon operations for a legacy greeting card company that had never sold on the marketplace before. Instead of randomly uploading products and hoping for the best, he started with proven retail winners, used Helium 10 to identify high-value keywords, and built his launch strategy around branded search, long-tail keyword opportunities, and strong catalog positioning. The episode gets tactical with Spencer’s Amazon launch process, including how he uses test listings to check Amazon relevancy before launching a real SKU, why he relies on Amazon Vine for early reviews, and how he structures PPC campaigns for ranking momentum. He also breaks down his use of branded campaigns, product targeting, long-tail exact match keywords, fixed bids, and bid rules that raise or lower bids based on sponsored and organic rank. Bradley and Spencer also d...

items.title: #752 - This PPC Launch Strategy Blew My Mind
items.excerpt: An Amazon seller from Singapore reveals how data-backed product research, AI workflows, Cerebro keywords, and a wild 50 auto campaign PPC strategy helped scale Amazon products to seven figures.
items.body: An Amazon seller from Singapore reveals how data-backed product research, AI workflows, Cerebro keywords, and a wild 50 auto campaign PPC strategy helped scale Amazon products to seven figures. ► Watch The Podcasts On Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/@Helium10SeriousSellersPodcast?sub_confirmation=1 ► Instagram: instagram.com/serioussellerspodcast ► Free Amazon Seller Chrome Extension: https://h10.me/extension ► Sign Up For Helium 10: https://h10.me/signup (Use SSP10 To Save 10% For Life) ► Learn How To Sell on Amazon: https://h10.me/ft What does it take to walk away from a stable career, bet on Amazon FBA, and build a multiple seven-figure business in the Amazon US marketplace from Singapore? In this episode, Bradley Sutton sits down with Clarence Cheang of TheFBABros to unpack the mindset, strategy, and data-driven systems that have driven his Amazon journey. Clarence shares how he went from a civil engineer working on Singapore’s infrastructure projects to a full-time entrepreneur after setting a clear goal: to make his side hustle outperform his day job income for 6 straight months. Clarence breaks down the product research process that helped him discover one of his breakthrough products: a Japanese egg pan that went on to generate over $1 million in lifetime sales. Instead of blindly chasing trends, he uses a highly analytical process built around 23 data points, competitor keyword weaknesses, and Helium 10 Cerebro research. His approach focuses on finding products where competitors may be making strong revenue but are weak in PPC, keyword ranking, or listing optimization. The conversation also dives into how AI is changing the way sellers research, launch, and scale. Clarence explains how AI can reduce product validation from one or two hours down to just minute...
